g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain how pandas reads CSV files in model validation')
g.text: df = pd.read_csv(filepath) → reads CSV, splits into X (features) and y (target/answer)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why do we split data into train and test sets?')
g.text: train_test_split() → 80% used to TEACH the model
20% kept hidden to TEST if it really learned

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a RandomForestClassifier and how does it work?')
g.text: RandomForestClassifier learns patterns from training data → model.fit(X_train, y_train)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain accuracy check in model validation simply')
sendPrompt('Explain precision recall and F1 score simply with example')
sendPrompt('What is overfitting in machine learning explain simply')
sendPrompt('What is a confusion matrix explain with simple example')
sendPrompt('What happens when model validation passes or fails?')
g.text: Accuracy ≥ 80% → PASS | F1 ≥ 0.75 → PASS | Overfit gap < 15% → PASS
confusion_matrix.png saved in your folder — visual map of where AI made mistakes
